free_text: SCRIPPS INST.OF OCEANOGRAPHY+SCHOOL OF
SCIENCE+ENGINEERING.
SOME CROSS SECTIONS FOR THE PRODUCTION OF RADIO-
NUCLIDES IN THE BOMBARDMENT OF C,N,O,AND FE BY MEDIUM
ENERGY PROTONS
TARGETS WERE PREPARED BY MIXING WITH A FEW PERCENT OF
ALUMINIUM DUST TO PERMIT RELIABLE MONITORING OF BEAM.
POLYETHYLENE, SEMICARBAZIDE, PLEXIGLAS,AND ALUMINIUM
LACTATE PROVIDED CROSS SECTIONS IN CARBON AND OXYGEN.
IRON TARGETS FOR THE MEASUREMENT OF SHORT-LIVED
ACTIVITIES WERE PREPARED BY STACKING THIN SHEETS OF
IRON AND ALUMINIUM FOILS OF THICKNESSES 19.8 AND 6.52
MG/CM2 RESPECTIVELY; FOR THE MEASUREMENT OF LONG LIVED,
A COLD ROLLED STEEL OF DIMENSIONS 5*5*0.15 CM3.
THE STANDARD DEVIATIONS WERE +- 10% FOR BE-7 CROSS
SECTIONS, +- 16% FOR H-3 CROSS SECTIONS. STATISTICAL
ERRORS FOR CROSS SECTIONS IN IORN BOMBARDMENTS WERE
ESTIMATED TO BE ABOUT 20% FOR NUCLIDES CL-32M AND
NA-22; REMAINING CASES, THE ERRORS WERE SMALLER AND
EXPECTED TO LIE BETWEEN 10 AND 15%.
DATA TAKEN FROM PR,118,1618,1960--TABLE I,II AND TEXT.
BIB UPDATE
